summary: Drew DeVault's April 2019 status update covers progress across his many projects, with aerc2 (a terminal email client) as the highlight. The email client now supports reading emails with embedded terminal emulation, configurable vim-style keybindings, and rendering HTML email through TUI browsers. He also launched git-send-email.io to teach email-based git workflows, worked on lists.sr.ht improvements driven by Alpine Linux's potential migration, continued Sway/Wayland development including a Godot Wayland backend, built a self-hosted livestreaming platform, and started cozy, an ambitious C compiler toolchain project.
key_points: aerc2 now reads emails with embedded terminal emulation, configurable keybindings, and HTML rendering via w3m
git-send-email.io launched as an interactive tutorial promoting email-based git workflows used by major FOSS projects
Alpine Linux is evaluating migration to lists.sr.ht, driving improvements in moderation, access controls, and MTA support
A Wayland backend for the Godot game engine is nearly complete, bringing native Wayland support to Godot-based games
New projects include a self-hosted livestreaming platform built on free software and cozy, a from-scratch C compiler toolchain
money_quotes: It can now read emails, including filtering them through arbitrary commands for highlighting diffs or coloring quotes, or even rendering HTML email with a TUI browser like w3m.

This workflow is the one sourcehut focuses on, and is also used by a large number of important free software projects, like Linux, gcc, clang, glibc, musl, ffmpeg, vim, emacs, coreutils… and many, many more.
Alpine Linux has provisioned some infrastructure for a likely migration from their current mailing list solution to one based on lists.sr.ht.
I also have been working more on Godot, and have switched gears towards adding a Wayland backend to Godot upstream - so you can play Godot-based video games on Wayland.

Once upon a time I was livestreaming programming sessions on Twitch.tv, and in the future I'd like to do this more often on my new platform. This one is open source and built on the shoulders of free software tools.
It's kind of ambitious… it's a full end-to-end C compiler toolchain.
key_insight: DeVault's prolific output across email clients, mailing list infrastructure, Wayland compositors, game engine backends, livestreaming, and compiler toolchains reflects a consistent mission to replace proprietary platforms with free software alternatives built from the ground up.
